title: tf.keras.layers.MaxPool2D  |  TensorFlow v2.16.1
description:
Max pooling operation for 2D spatial data.
Inherits From:Layer,Operation
Main aliasestf.keras.layers.MaxPooling2D

parameters.name: pool_size
parameters.description:
Downsamples the input along its spatial dimensions (height and width)
by taking the maximum value over an input window
(of size defined by) for each channel of the input.
The window is shifted bystrid

parameters.name: strides
parameters.description:
Downsamples the input along its spatial dimensions (height and width)
by taking the maximum value over an input window
(of size defined bypool_size) for each channel of the input.
The window is shifte

parameters.name: "valid"
parameters.description:
The resulting output when using thepadding option has a spatial
shape (number of rows or columns) of:output_shape = math.floor((input_shape - pool_size) / strides) + 1(wheninput_shape >= pool_size)

parameters.name: input_shape >= pool_size
parameters.description:
The resulting output when using the"valid"padding option has a spatial
shape (number of rows or columns) of:output_shape = math.floor((input_shape - pool_size) / strides) + 1(when)

parameters.name: "same"
parameters.description: The resulting output shape when using thepadding option is:output_shape = math.floor((input_shape - 1) / strides) + 1

parameters.name: pool_size
parameters.description:
int or tuple of 2 integers, or None. Strides values. If None,
it will default to. If only one int is specified, the
same stride size will be used for all dimensions.

string, eitheror"same"(case-insensitive)."valid"means no padding."same"results in padding evenly to
